chunks.content:
Article: What Was Anno Doing
[]: What Was Anno Doing?             Quote Post by TheBlueTree » Fri Jul 08, 2005 10:53 am Does anyone really understand what point Anno was trying to get through to us? I mean like what morals or like stuff was he trying to show to us? Don't build giant robots and turn people to LCL?(joke) Was he trying to show us to not run away (like Shinji always does), or make us try to understand that everyone can hurt everyone, but we gata try to make the best of it... or like what is the message, really?

[]:             Quote Post by ice reaper » Fri Jul 08, 2005 11:07 am don't get obsessed with something which eventually drains all of the time out of your short insignificant life? oops, too late

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Fri Jul 08, 2005 12:05 pm "The real world is painful and kind of crappy, but don't set it aside to live in a fantasy". In other words, remember that your life is worthwhile, and make sure to enjoy it, and make sure to care for the people around you.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryo » Fri Jul 08, 2005 1:08 pm I think it's a lot of message's actually. - Anywhere can be heaven, as long as you have the will to live. - We need others to define ourselves. The one that Soluzar mentioned in the above post is probably one of the most important.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Fri Jul 08, 2005 3:20 pm Don't forget this one: -"Anime is sucking." Really.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Jul 08, 2005 4:13 pm "There's always hope. Don't run away."

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Fri Jul 08, 2005 4:49 pm "jerking off over your comatose friend's body is a bad idea"

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Fri Jul 08, 2005 5:01 pm Shinji & Asuka are fucked up, and if even they can make the choice to go on living we have no real excuse to not.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Bud » Fri Jul 08, 2005 6:53 pm "The trendiest anime genre of the 80's mixed with the trendiest religion of the 90's, equals one hell of a money maker... (And teenage angst thrown in doesn't hurt either.)"

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Jul 08, 2005 8:39 pm "Making an anime with a shoestring budget is stressful, but it's a very effective form of self-therapy."
